current.summary: Reviewed benchmark installer implementation plan — assessed current state, all 4 layers are unstarted. Creating handoff for future execution.

activeWork.description: Implement --to-all flag and benchmark skill installer workflow
activeWork.progress: Plan is fully written with 4 layers and 14 tasks. No code or docs written yet. Layer 1 (--to-all flag) is the critical path — 8 tasks covering TDD tests, flag registration, mutual-exclusion validation, helper extraction, runInstallToAll implementation, regression suite, smoke test, and commit. Layers 2-4 follow after.

git.lastCommit: a634fa5 docs: move ACP spec to docs/spec/acp/ with LICENSE

recentDecisions: Confirmed all 4 layers of the benchmark installer plan are unstarted — no partial work exists
Existing multi-provider loadout beads (syllago-5dwo, syllago-ohdb) are related but not the specific roadmap bead from Task 4.1
nextSteps: Execute Layer 1: Start with Task 1.1 (write failing tests for --to-all) in cli/cmd/syllago/install_cmd_test.go
Execute Layer 1 Tasks 1.2-1.7: Flag registration, validation, helper extraction, runInstallToAll, full test suite, smoke test
Execute Layer 1 Task 1.8: Commit with 'feat(install): add --to-all flag for multi-provider install'
Execute Layer 2: Manual benchmark registry setup and verification (depends on Layer 1)
Execute Layers 3-4: Workflow doc and roadmap bead (independent of Layer 2)

context.notes: The implementation plan is very detailed with exact code blocks, file paths, and success criteria for each task. Follow it closely — it was designed for TDD (tests first in Task 1.1, implementation in 1.2-1.5). Key files: cli/cmd/syllago/install_cmd.go (implementation), cli/cmd/syllago/install_cmd_test.go (tests). The plan removes MarkFlagRequired('to') from cobra and replaces it with runtime validation in RunE — this is necessary for the mutual-exclusion logic to work. Watch for regressions in existing TestInstall* tests after removing MarkFlagRequired.
